mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25pp.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and impending corporate earnings declines, which forces equity markets to price in lower forward profitability over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and capital demand, which reduces aggregate corporate revenue growth and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60d returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports near-term corporate output growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns in >70% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 205 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >55% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 205 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand strength, which validates corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive equity market performance over the following month.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS expansion signal produces negative 20-day returns in >60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices above 110 signal robust manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive short-term equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels above 110 consistently precede negative 5-day returns in >50% of observations.
